### Post by GregaS on 2006-06-27

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by tkmn on 2006-06-27
You need this line in *xorg.conf*

```
Section "Module"
        Load    "i2c"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "type1"
        Load    "vbe"
**        Load    "fglrx"**
EndSection

```

---

### Post by jecos on 2006-06-27
[QUOTE=tkmn]You need this line in *xorg.conf*

```
Section "Module"
        Load    "i2c"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "type1"
        Load    "vbe"
**        Load    "fglrx"**
EndSection

```[/QUOTE]

No thats not the correct way for fglrx to be loaded. It will load automatically,
**DO NOT** ADD THAT TO YOUR xorg.conf

"sudo aticonfig --intial" to verify that your config is ok.

Are you using the ubuntu supplied driver, or ati-installer?

---

### Post by Mau on 2006-06-27
I too upgraded from a functioning setup and it no longer works.  I added the "load" in my xorg.conf file, but fglrxinfo still says MESA project.

---

### Post by jecos on 2006-06-27
[QUOTE=Mau]I too upgraded from a functioning setup and it no longer works.  I added the "load" in my xorg.conf file, but fglrxinfo still says MESA project.[/QUOTE]

Make sure you have linux-686 or linux-k7 meta package installed, to make sure your restricted-modules are there.


GUYS/GALS PLEASE SPECIFY IF YOU ARE USING THE UBUNTU SUPPLIED DRIVER, OR A NEWER ONE AND WHICH ONE?

---

### Post by Mau on 2006-06-27
I used the Ubuntu updater (apt-get).

Both of those meta packages are not installed.  Should I install them?  It's prompting to load nvidia drivers and linux images. ??  The driver that I installed is the one that Kubuntu just prompted to download.

---

### Post by jecos on 2006-06-27
[QUOTE=Mau]I used the Ubuntu updater (apt-get).

Both of those meta packages are not installed.  Should I install them?  It's prompting to load nvidia drivers and linux images. ??[/QUOTE]

It will install the restricted modules, which includes fglrx, nvidia, wireless modules.. etc.  If you don't have them installed fglrx will not work.

YOU ONLY INSTALL ONE, either linux-686 for intel or linux-k7 for amd. (INSTALL THE METAPACKAGE SO IF AN UPDATED KERNEL COMES IT WILL UPDATE TO IT!)

---

### Post by The Devil Is A Squirrel on 2006-06-27
I don't know if it really helps you but I followed [this]("http://wiki.ubuntu.com/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI") guide with the little hint from me if it not works ```
The following steps did not work for me. In exchange to the next steps enter  'dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg' and choose 'fglrx'.
```

On my machine it works now just fine...

### Post by GregaS on 2006-06-27
I have linux-image-686 and linux-restricted-686 and it still reports as MESA. I also tried sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg and chose fglrx and it still reports as MESA. This is just crazy. I never had any problems with this.

PS: "sudo aticonfig --intial" returns this:

> aticonfig: unrecognized option `--intial'
aticonfig: parsing the command-line failed.

EDIT: Found out that it should be "sudo aticonfig initial"

This returns:

> Using /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Saved back-up to /etc/X11/xorg.conf.fglrx-5
